description:
You are a deployment engineer preparing a new roll-out.  
A helper script later in the pipeline expects the list of target hosts to be provided as a valid Bash array that looks exactly like this example:

DEPLOY_TARGETS=("hostA" "hostB" "hostC")

Your job now is to transform an existing plain-text file into that array format.

1. Source file  
   • Path: /home/user/deploy/targets.txt  
   • Format: one host name per line, no surrounding spaces.  
     (The file already exists. Do NOT modify it.)

2. Required output file  
   • Path: /home/user/deploy/targets_array.sh  
   • It must contain EXACTLY one line, followed by a single trailing newline.  
   • That single line must start with the literal token DEPLOY_TARGETS=(, then list every host from targets.txt in the **same order**, each host enclosed in double-quotes and separated by a single space, and finally close with a right parenthesis ).  
   • Example for three hosts “web-01”, “api-01”, “db-01”:  
     DEPLOY_TARGETS=("web-01" "api-01" "db-01")

3. Log file for verification  
   • After creating targets_array.sh, append a line that reads exactly SUCCESS to the file /home/user/deploy/transform.log (create the log file if it does not yet exist).  
   • The word SUCCESS must be in all caps and be the only content on its line.

Important technical notes  
• Fulfil the entire transformation with a single shell command (piping and redirection are allowed, but run it as one compound command).  
• Do not place additional text, comments, or blank lines in either output file.  
• You do not need root privileges; everything resides under /home/user.  
• The automated tests will 1) read /home/user/deploy/targets_array.sh and 2) look for the literal word SUCCESS as the last non-empty line of /home/user/deploy/transform.log.  
• Write any shell commands you decide to use directly in the terminal; do not embed them in a script.

Once both files meet the exact format described above, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial state (pre-populated before the agent starts):

1. Directory /home/user/deploy exists with normal user write permissions.
2. File /home/user/deploy/targets.txt contains exactly four lines and a final newline:
web-01
web-02
api-01
db-01

3. The files /home/user/deploy/targets_array.sh and /home/user/deploy/transform.log do NOT yet exist.

Expected final state:

1. /home/user/deploy/targets_array.sh exists, is a regular text file, and its exact byte content (including the trailing newline) is:
DEPLOY_TARGETS=("web-01" "web-02" "api-01" "db-01")
␊

2. /home/user/deploy/transform.log exists.  
   • Its final line (last non-empty line) is the literal text:
SUCCESS

   • If the agent writes additional informational lines above it, that is acceptable; only the presence of the final SUCCESS line will be asserted.

No other files or directories should be changed or created.
